level_signals: Comp range $365K-$435K base, consistent with senior IC at Anthropic
'Several years of experience in technical program management' — vague but implies seasoned professional
Mandate spans cross-region, multi-cloud, and hybrid cloud strategies for critical infrastructure
External vendor and hyperscaler relationship ownership at working level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have several years of experience in technical program management, with a track record of successfully delivering complex, cross-functional programs
Have experience with database, storage, or core infrastructure programs and can engage meaningfully with the engineers who design and operate them
Have managed external vendor or cloud provider relationships at the working level, including commitments, escalations, and joint roadmaps
Are highly organized and can manage multiple parallel workstreams effectively across distributed teams
Thrive in unstructured environments with a knack for bringing order to chaos and creating clarity in ambiguous situations
Have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority
Are passionate about Anthropic's mission and interested in the challenges of operating AI infrastructure safely and reliably
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have experience with cross-region, multi-cloud, or hybrid cloud infrastructure (preferred but not required)

scope.surface: Database infrastructure underpinning Claude, Anthropic APIs, and internal systems — spanning cross-region resilience, multi-cloud operations, and private/public/hybrid cloud footprint
scope.business_model: internal platform / infrastructure (supports B2C and B2B products)
primary_metrics_inferred: Successful delivery of cross-region, multi-cloud, and hybrid cloud programs
core_responsibilities: Own the roadmap and execution across cross-region, multi-cloud, and private/public/hybrid cloud strategy pillars
Coordinate across database vendors, hyperscalers, internal platform teams, and infrastructure teams
Manage external vendor and hyperscaler relationships at the working level: commitments, escalations, and joint roadmaps
Drive dependencies, risks, and tradeoffs for databases as critical infrastructure
Create program documentation including roadmaps, status reports, risk assessments, and communication plans
Facilitate technical decision-making by bringing together stakeholders, driving consensus, and ensuring timely resolution of blocking issues

compensation.equity_form: optional equity donation matching mentioned (suggests options/RSUs are part of package)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($365K-$435K base)
Clear mandate and three concrete strategy pillars — not vague
Explicitly encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Cross-region/multi-cloud experience listed as preferred, not required — lowers barrier to entry
red_flags: Years of experience not quantified ('several years') — internal leveling may be opaque to candidates
Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Very broad coordination scope (database vendors, hyperscalers, internal platform, infrastructure) suggests high complexity and potential for stakeholder overload

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and program scope; not stated explicitly)
seniority.years_required_min (JD says 'several years' but gives no numeric floor; left null)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and success definition; not stated verbatim)
location.cities_listed (inferred from HQ reference; no explicit city stated in role section)
ambiguities: 'Several years of experience' is intentionally vague; no numeric minimum is provided, making it difficult to assess hard vs. soft threshold.

Cross-region/multi-cloud experience is explicitly called 'preferred but not required' — classified as nice_to_have despite appearing in the 'You may be a good fit if you' section alongside apparent hard requirements.
Location cities not explicitly named for this role; San Francisco inferred from company HQ reference in JD footer.
